title: The Finally Block 🧹
description: Guarantee cleanup code runs, even if the world is burning.

slides.title: The Cleanup Crew 🧹
slides.content:
# Messy Situations

Imagine you try to open a file on the computer. Before reading, you `lock` the file so nobody else can edit it.
Suddenly... the reading process crashes!

Execution jumps to the `catch` block... but wait, the file is STILL locked! You never unlocked it!
This causes catastrophic memory leaks.

slides.title: Always Runs ⏱️

quizData.question: If the `try` block succeeds without any errors whatsoever, does the `finally` block execute?

options.text: Yes, it always executes no matter what.
No, it only executes if there was a catch.

quizData.explanation: Finally represents guaranteed execution. It runs upon success AND upon failure.

quizData.question: If you open a database connection in a try block, you should guarantee closing it by placing the close() command inside the _______ block.
quizData.explanation: You use finally to always clean up and release resources.
